title: श्री कृष्ण स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कृष्ण स्तुति ॥

कृष्णाय वासुदेवाय हरये परमात्मने।
प्रणतक्लेशनाशाय गोविन्दाय नमो नमः॥ १॥

नमः पङ्कजनाभाय नमः पङ्कजमालिने।
नमः पङ्कजनेत्राय नमस्ते पङ्कजाङ्घ्रये॥ २॥

वनमाली गदी शार्ङ्गी शङ्खी चक्री च नन्दकी।
श्रीमान् नारायणो विष्णुर्वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु॥ ३॥

देवकीसुतं गोविन्दं वासुदेवं जगत्पतिम्।
देहिमे तनयं कृष्ण त्वामहं शरणं गतः॥ ४॥

नमामि यमुनातीरवनमालाविभूषितम्।
मुरलीगानलोलं तं वन्दे श्रीनन्दनन्दनम्॥ ५॥

पीताम्बरधरं देवं कुण्डलालङ्कृतप्रभम्।
नन्दगोपकुमारं तं वन्दे गोकुलनायकम्॥ ६॥

गोपीजनवल्लभं गोपालं गोवर्धनोद्धारम्।
कालियमर्दनं कृष्णं वन्दे गोकुलरक्षकम्॥ ७॥

राधाप्रियं मधुसूदनं मनोहरं
कंसध्वंसिनं शूरसेनतनयप्रभम्।
श्रीवत्सलाञ्छनं श्रीपतिमद्वयं
कृष्णं वन्दे देवकीनन्दनम्॥ ८॥

मथुरानाथमाद्यन्तरहितं
मधुराधिपतिं सकलार्तिहरम्।
द्वारकावासिनं जगदीश्वरं
कृष्णं वन्दे शरणागतवत्सलम्॥ ९॥

यशोदानन्दनं बालं गोपवेषधरं हरिम्।
मक्खनचोरं मनोहारिणं वन्दे कृष्णं सनातनम्॥ १०॥

क्षीरसागरशायिनं लक्ष्मीकान्तमनाथरक्षकम्।
विश्वव्यापिनमीशं परात्परं कृष्णं प्रणमामि सर्वदा॥ ११॥

कृष्णस्तुतिमिमां दिव्यां यः पठेत् प्रयतः शुचिः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥ १२॥

॥ इति श्री कृष्ण स्तुति सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Krishna Stuti ||

Krishnaya Vasudevaya Haraye Paramatmane.
Pranata-klesha-nashaya Govindaya namo namah. || 1 ||

Namah Pankaja-nabhaya namah Pankaja-maline.
Namah Pankaja-netraya namas-te Pankaja-anghraye. || 2 ||

Vanamali Gadi Sharangi Shankhi Chakri cha Nandaki.
Shriman Narayano Vishnur-Vasudevo-abhirakshatu. || 3 ||

Devaki-sutam Govindam Vasudevam Jagat-patim.
Dehi me tanayam Krishna tvam-aham sharanam gatah. || 4 ||

Namami Yamuna-tira-vanamala-vibhushitam.
Murali-gana-lolam tam vande Shri Nanda-nandanam. || 5 ||

Pitambara-dharam devam Kundala-alankrita-prabham.
Nanda-gopa-kumaram tam vande Gokula-nayakam. || 6 ||

Gopi-jana-vallabham Gopalam Govardhana-uddharam.
Kaliya-mardanam Krishnam vande Gokula-rakshakam. || 7 ||

Radha-priyam Madhusudanam manoharam
Kamsa-dhvamsinam Shurasena-tanaya-prabham.
Shrivatsa-lanchhanam Shri-patim-advayam
Krishnam vande Devaki-nandanam. || 8 ||

Mathura-natham-adyanta-rahitam
Madhura-adhipatim sakala-arti-haram.
Dvaraka-vasinam Jagadishvaram
Krishnam vande Sharanagata-vatsalam. || 9 ||

Yashoda-nandanam balam gopa-vesha-dharam Harim.
Makkhana-choram mano-harinam vande Krishnam sanatanam. || 10 ||

Kshira-sagara-shayinam Lakshmi-kantam-anatha-rakshakam.
Vishva-vyapinam-isham paratparam Krishnam pranamami sarvada. || 11 ||

Krishna-stutim-imam divyam yah pathet prayatah shuchih.
Sarva-papa-vinimurkto Vishnu-lokam sa gachchhati. || 12 ||

|| Iti Shri Krishna Stuti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
कृष्ण को, वासुदेव को, हरि को, परमात्मा को, शरणागतों के क्लेशों का नाश करने वाले गोविन्द को नमस्कार॥ १॥

कमलनाभ को नमस्कार, कमलमाला धारी को नमस्कार, कमलनेत्र को नमस्कार, कमल जैसे चरणों वाले को नमस्कार॥ २॥

वनमाली, गदाधारी, शार्ङ्गधारी, शंखधारी, चक्रधारी, नन्दक तलवार धारी श्रीमान नारायण विष्णु वासुदेव रक्षा करें॥ ३॥

यमुना तट पर वनमाला से विभूषित, मुरली गान में लीन श्री नन्दनन्दन को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ५॥

पीताम्बर धारण करने वाले, कुण्डलों से सुशोभित, नन्द गोप के कुमार, गोकुल के नायक को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ॥ ६॥

गोपीजनवल्लभ, गोपाल, गोवर्धन उठाने वाले, कालिय नाग का मर्दन करने वाले, गोकुल के रक्षक कृष्ण को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ॥ ७॥

राधा के प्रिय, मधुसूदन, मनोहर, कंसध्वंसी, श्रीवत्स लांछन धारी, अद्वय श्रीपति देवकीनन्दन कृष्ण को वन्दन करता हूँ॥ ८॥

यशोदा नन्दन, बालक, गोपवेषधारी, माखनचोर, मनहरने वाले सनातन कृष्ण को वन्दन करता हूँ॥ १०॥

इस दिव्य कृष्ण स्तुति को जो पवित्र होकर पढ़ता है वह सर्व पापों से मुक्त होकर विष्णुलोक जाता है॥ १२॥
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of all sorrows and afflictions of the devotee
Invokes the blessing of the blessings of lord krishna in all forms
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and purifies the soul
Ideal for daily recitation for Krishna devotees
description: Krishna Stuti (श्री कृष्ण स्तुति) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Krishna. Rooted in Traditional Sanskrit Stotra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant peace, prosperity, and happiness as well as destroy all sorrows and afflictions of the devotee. Additionally, it is known to remove all sins and purify the soul. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Janmashtami and Holi to invoke divine grace. With 12 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Krishna Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Krishna Stuti in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Twelve verses praising Lord Krishna's divine forms and pastimes.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Krishna Stuti and where does it come from?
seoFaqs.answer: The Krishna Stuti is a short twelve-verse Sanskrit hymn praising Lord Krishna in his many forms — Vasudeva, Govinda, Nanda-nandana, Madhusudana, Mathuranatha and Dvarakavasi. The opening verse Krishnaya Vasudevaya Haraye Paramatmane is one of the most widely recited Krishna invocations in classical tradition and appears in several stotra collections drawn from Puranic and traditional Sanskrit sources. The composition gathers Krishna's principal nama-stutis into a single recitation, making it a popular daily prayer for Vaishnava devotees and a standard inclusion in Janmashtami liturgies. (Sources: stotra-nidhi.com; sanskritdocuments.org; vedadhara.com Krishna stotras.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Krishna Stuti and a Krishna Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: In Sanskrit, both stuti and stotra mean a hymn of praise, but stuti generally refers to a shorter, more emotional outpouring of devotion, while stotra is often a more formal composition with a fixed structure, viniyoga, dhyana and phalashruti. Many famous Krishna prayers are titled stuti — Kunti Stuti from the Bhagavata Purana, Brahma Stuti, Akrura Stuti, Arjuna Stuti — and are spoken in moments of crisis or surrender by characters in the narrative. The Krishna Stuti on this page is a compact garland-style hymn that focuses on praise and surrender rather than detailed mantra-vidhi. (Sources: classical Sanskrit stotra literature; Bhagavata Purana commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Krishna Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Krishna Stuti is traditionally recited on Wednesday and Friday, the days associated with Krishna and Vishnu, and as part of daily morning sandhya for Vaishnava households. It is especially recommended on Janmashtami midnight, on Ashtami and Ekadashi tithis of every paksha, during the month of Kartik, and on Holi when Krishna's pastimes with Radha and the gopis are remembered. The compact length of twelve verses makes it suitable for inclusion at the start of any Krishna puja, or as a closing stuti after lengthier recitations such as the Vishnu Sahasranama or Bhagavad Gita parayana. (Sources: drikpanchang.com Janmashtami; Vaishnava panchang traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Krishna Stuti promise to its reciter?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti at the close of the stuti — sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnu-lokam sa gachchhati — promises that one who recites this divine hymn with purity and devotion is freed from all sins and attains Vishnuloka (Vaikuntha) after death. Within the verses themselves, devotees seek protection from Krishna in his weapon-bearing Narayana form, the gift of a virtuous son (dehi me tanayam Krishna), removal of afflictions through the grace of Govinda, and the simple bliss of remembering Krishna's pastimes at Yamuna, Govardhana, Mathura and Dvaraka. (Sources: Puranic stotra collections; vedadhara.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does the stuti name so many different forms of Krishna?
seoFaqs.answer: The Krishna Stuti deliberately invokes the lila-vibhuti — the playful manifestations of Krishna across his life. The verses move geographically from Vrindavan (Yamuna-tira, Murali-gana, Nanda-nandana, Gopala, Govardhana-uddharaka, Kaliya-mardana) to Mathura (Kamsa-dhvamsi, Mathura-natha) to Dvaraka (Dvarakavasi, Jagadishvara) and finally back to the cosmic ocean (Kshira-sagara-shayi, Lakshmi-kanta). This sequence allows the devotee to meditate on Krishna both as the intimate boy of Vrindavan and as the supreme Para-Brahman, capturing the bhakti tradition's insight that the same Krishna is both the most accessible and the most transcendent reality. (Sources: traditional Krishna theology; Bhagavata Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: Can Krishna Stuti be recited by anyone, or are there qualifications?
seoFaqs.answer: There are no caste, gender or age restrictions on the recitation of Krishna Stuti. The phalashruti specifies only that the reciter should be prayatah shuchih — sincere and clean in body and mind. Krishna bhakti tradition, as expounded in the Bhagavad Gita (9.32) and Bhagavata Purana, is explicitly open to all sincere devotees regardless of birth. Children are often taught the opening verse Krishnaya Vasudevaya Haraye Paramatmane as their first Krishna prayer, and the entire hymn is recited by households across India during Janmashtami without any pre-qualification. (Sources: Bhagavad Gita 9.32; classical Vaishnava acharya commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Pankaja-nabhaya verse repeated in many stutis?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse Namah pankaja-nabhaya namah pankaja-maline namah pankaja-netraya namaste pankaja-anghraye is one of the most beloved Vishnu-Krishna invocations in Sanskrit literature. It salutes Krishna as the One whose navel bears the lotus from which Brahma was born (creator), whose chest wears a lotus garland (sustainer), whose eyes are lotus-like (the all-seeing witness), and whose feet are lotus-soft (the refuge of the surrendered). The same verse appears prominently in the Kunti Stuti of the Bhagavata Purana and in many other Vaishnava prayers, indicating its status as a foundational Krishna mantra that Vaishnava acharyas have woven into multiple stotras. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 1.8.22; Vaishnava nitya-puja paddhati.)
